lines.tx: If you're fighting cancer just like me, this song is for you
I'm Maja and this is my third relapse, and I have every reason to diss you
So what do you say, Maja? Let's go at him! Let's go!
Hahaha, let's go then!
I've beaten you once before, I remember it like yesterday
I thought it was over, that you wouldn't come back here, so
I tasted victory, my eyes shone with the whole world
And you came back again and said: once more!
Third round of the fight, you put me back at the start
But you forgot that I won't give up just like that
Though this road is hard and sometimes I feel weak
I often see my mom wiping away her tears, I love you
Still here, thought you had me?
Still here, we're laughing in your face
Still here and I'm not going anywhere
Still here! Maja, give it to this loser!
This is your last step, I'll prove it to you
Listen up, cancer, because your days are numbered
You attack children – that is a true disgrace
You steal our dreams and our childhood fantasies
Every time I fall, it teaches me to move forward
The scars on my body are badges of many roads
Did you think you'd break me, that you'd take away my days?
Unfortunately you were wrong – get out, I'll close the door!
Still here, thought you had me?
Still here, we're laughing in your face
Still here and I'm not going anywhere
Still here! Maja, give it to this loser!
You latched onto little Maja? Feeding off other people's dreams?
Pardon my language, but I think it's been ages since anyone—, Bedi!
We'll go for mani-pedi with Maja, our fans right beside us, when we're always dancing and always laughing
Right after we absolutely—, Bedi, come on!
You took my dad, then you took my grandpa
You wanted to take my mom, but she wouldn't let you, crazy thing
Leszek writes this verse for me, Sara throws me the words
Because even when you win, heaven gains an angel
This is for everyone on the wards, where everyone fights
Like soldiers from the special forces
Nurses, doctors – men and women – and parents:
We won't lose this fight, Maja!
Still here
Still here
Still here
Still here
